content.full_text:
Farn Polrrrcar- PRACTTcBs C oMMrssroN
P.O. Box 807 c 428 J Street o Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 . Fax (9t6) 322-0886
March 12,2002
Tei Yukimoto
Deputy City Attorney
City of Fresno
2600 Fresno Street
Fresno, CA 93721-3602
Re:
Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-02-031
Dear Ms. Yukimoto
This letter is in response to your request on behalf of Georgeanne White, for
advice regarding the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the
"Act").1 This letter is limited solely to the provisions of the Act and should not be taken
as advice or an opinion regarding any other area ofthe law potentially raised by your
letter.
Since your request does not concern a specific pending decision, we are treating
your request as one for informal assistance. (Regulation 18329(c)(8XC) and (F).) 2 This
assistance is based upon the facts as provided in your letters. Please note that this letter
should not be construed to evaluate any conduct that has already taken place.
(Regulation 1 832e(b)(8)(A) )
QUESTION
Does Ms. Georgeanne White, Chief of Staffto the Mayor, City of Fresno, have a
disqualifying conflict-of-interest under the Act prohibiting her participation in
governmental decisions concerning the Regional Medical Center Project?
CONCLUSION
Ms. White may participate in decisions concerning the Regional Medicai Center
Project if there are no reasonably foreseeable material financial effects on her economic
interests.
I Government Code $$ 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, $$ 18109-
18997, of the California Code of Regulations. As used herein "sectiorf' refers to a section of the Act and
''regulation" refers to a section or subdivision of the Commission's regulations.
2 Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or
formal written advice. (Section 83 1 1 4 ; regulation 1 83 2 9(c)(3 ).)
'gE


File No. I-02-031
Page No. 2
FACTS
In 1995, the City of Fresno ("Fresno") and the Redevelopment Agency of the City
of Fresno ("Redevelopment Agency") entered into a development agreement and land
sale contract with Community Hospitals of Central California ("CHCC"), to develdp the
Regional Medical Center Project ("Project"). CHCC is a nonprofit corporation and a sole
member of Fresno Community Hospital and Medical Center ("Fresno CH&MC"), a non-
profit corporation, which operates three acute care hospitals. CHCC has assigned certain
of its development obligations under this agreement to Fresno CH&MC.
The development agreement calls for Fresno CH&MC to expand its facilities into
a rnulti-use hospital and a medical center complex within a 58 acre integrated regional
medical center campus. The development will take place in four phases. In the first
phase, the Redevelopment Agency will acquire and clear the site location and sell the
land to CHCC, subject to available city funds and the necessary findings pursuant to the
applicable legal procedures for exercising eminent domain. Fresno is to make certain
street improvements, including a street alignment and widening rights-of-way.
You state 3 that Ms. White provides administrative support duties for the Mayor.
Her duties are said to include planning, organizing, and overseeing the day-to-day
activities of the Mayor's office; acting as liaison between the Mayor, city management
staff, boards, commissions, and the public; working with consultants and management to
implement promotional activities; coordinating special projects and activities; and
interpreting city policies, rules, and regulations.
You further state that Ms. White's husband is an attorney and 4 percent partner in
a local, twenty-five member law firm. This law firm is retained by Norcal Mutual
Insurance Company ("Norcal") (apparently the largest physician-owned mutual insurance
company in Northern California) to provide medical malpractice insurance defense to the
acute care hospitals operated by Fresno CH&MC. The law firm receives payments for its
medial malpractice insurance defense services from Norcal, and not from CHCC or
Fresno CH&MC.
ANALYSIS
Section 87100 prohibits a public official from making, participating in making, or
otherwise using his or her official position to influence a governmental decision in which
the official has a financial interest. The Commission has adopted an eight-step standard
analysis for deciding whether an official has a disqualifying conflict-of-interest
(Regulation 18700, subdivisions (b)(t) - (8), which is discussed below. The general
rule, however, is that a conflict of interest may occur whenever a public official makes a
governmental decision which may reasonably be foreseen as materially affecting one or
more of his or her financial interests.
3 In a letter dated March 8,2002, you describe that Ms. White's duties specific to the Project will
be primzrily to act as a facilitator between Fresno city staff and CHCC to ensure that there will be no
delays in completing the Project under Fresno's existing policies and procedures.


File No. I-02-031
Page No. 3
1. 
Is Ms. White a public official?
The conflict of interest prohibition applies only to public officials. A public
official is broadly defined as every natural person who is a "member, officer, employee
or consultant of a state or local government agency". (Section 82048; regulation
18702(a).) Under the Act, the definition of "local government agency" includes a city .
(Section 82041.) Accordingly, as chief-of-staff to the Mayor of the City of Fresno,
Ms. White is an employee of the City and a public official within the meaning of the Act.
2. 
Will Ms. White make, participate in making, or influence a governmental
decision?
You describe Ms. White's duties as administrative support to the Mayor:
planning, organizing, overseeing day-to-day activities; acting as liaison, implementing
promotional activities, coordinating special projects, and interpreting City policies, rules
and regulations. It does not appear from your description that Ms. White will be involved
in making a governmental decision since her duties, above, do not involve obligating or
committing the city. (Section 87100; regulation 18702.1.) However, these duties will
cause her to directly advise or make recommendations to the Mayor, who is a public
official making governmental decisions. In this, she will be participating in making a
governmental decision. (Section 87100; regulation 18702.2.) Likewise, her duties
include influencing governmental decisions. A public offrcial is using his or her official
position to "influence a governmental decision" before his or her own agency if, for the
purpose of influencing a decision, the official contacts or appears before any member,
officer, employee, or consultant of his or her agency. (Section 87100; regulation
18702.3.)
3. What are Ms. White's economic interests?
The Act's conflict of interest provisions apply only to conflicts-of-interest arising
from economic interests. Under the facts provided, Ms. White's potential economic
interests which might give rise to a conflict-of- interest are:
i)
an economic interest in a business entity in which she has a direct or
indirect investment of $2,000 or more (section 87103(a); regulation
18703.1(a)); and
an economic interest in any source of income, including promised
income, which aggregates to $500 or more within 12 months prior to a
governmental decision (section 87103(c); regulation 1 8703.3).
iD
With regard to her potential economic interest in a business entity, the definition
of an indirect investment or interest includes any investment or interest owned by the
spouse or dependent child of a public official. (Section 87103.) For purposes of this


File No. I-02-031
Page No. 4
letter, we assume that the 4 percent interest of Ms. White's spouse in the law firm
represents an investment on his part of $2,000 or more.o
With regard to her spouse's income from the law partnership, Ms. White's
economic interest is based upon her community property interest in that income. (Section
82030(a).) It is reasonable to assume that the law partnership provided Ms. White's
spouse with an income aggregating $1,000 or more over the past l2-months. Ms.
White's community property interest in this income would meet the $500 or more
threshold under regulation 18703.3 and the law partnership would constitute a source of
income to her.
4. 
Will these economic interests be directly or indirectly involved?
A business entity or source of income in which a public official has an economic
interest is directly involved in a governmental decision if it initiates the proceeding by
filing an application, claim, appeal or similar request, or is a named party in, or is the
subject of the proceeding concerning the decision before the offlrcial or the official's
agency. (Regulation 18704.t(a)(t)-(a)(2).) If the business entity or source of income is
not directly involved, then it is deemed to be indirectly involved in the governmental
decision. (Regulation 18704.1(b) ) There are no facts shown indicating that the law firm
has or will initiate an action, become a named party, or become the subject of a
proceeding before the Fresno city government with respect to the Project. Insofar as
matters concerning the Project, Ms. White's economic interests are only indirectly
involved.
5.&6. Will there be a reasonably foreseeable material financial affect on one or
more of Ms. White's economic interests?
Not all governmental decisions by a public offrcial which impact upon his or her
economic interests give rise to a conflict-of-interest. It is when the impact on his or her
economic interests is material (or important)that a conflict may arise. Whether an
impact is so significant as to be deemed material depends upon the nature of the
economic interest and whether that interest is directly or indirectly implicated in the
governmental decision.
For indirectly involved business entities, including indirectly involved business
entities that are sources of income, the materiality standard varies according to the size of
the business entity and impact of the governmental decision upon the business entity's
financial status. (Regulations 1 8705. I and 18705.3.) As it pertains to Ms. White's
economic interest, the appropriate standard appears to be that applicable to business
entities that are not listed with the Fortune 500, not listed or meeting the financial criteria
to be listed on the New York Stock Exchange, or not listed or meeting the financial
crrterra to be listed on the NASDAQ/AMEX -- regulation 18705.1(c)(a):
o In some circumstances, a public official may also have an economic interest in sources of
incotne to a business in r,vhich he or she has a economic interests. Hor,vever, sources of income to the law
firm, including Norcal, are not economic interests of Ms. White because her spouse's ownership interest in
the law firm is less than l0 percent. (Section 82030(a).)


File No. l-02-031
Page No. 5
". . the financial effect of a governmental decision on the business entity is
material if it is reasonably foreseeable that:
(A) The governmental decision will result in an increase or decrease in the
business entity's gross revenues for a fiscal year in the amount of $20,000 or
more; or,
(B) The governmental decision will result in the business entity incurring or
avoiding additional expenses or reducing or eliminating existing expenses for
a fiscal year in the amount of $5,000 or more; or,
(C) The governmental decision will result in an increase or decrease in the value
of the business entity's assets or liabilities of $20,000 or more."
However, under regulation 18706, an effect upon economic interests is considered
reasonably foreseeable only if there is a substantial likelihood thatit will occur. A
financial effect need not be certain to be considered reasonably foreseeable, but it must
be more than a mere possibility. (In re Thorner, I FPPC Ops. 198 (1975).)
There are no facts provided in your letter to indicate that the law firm will be
affected in any of the above manners as a result of the decisions which the City of Fresno
will make with respect to the Project. Provided the governmental decisions by the City of
Fresno concerning the Project will not have a reasonably foreseeable material financial
effect upon Ms. White's economic interest in the law firm, Ms. White will not have a
disqualifying conflict of interest and may participate in those decisions.
The Commission's publication "Can I Vote" is enclosed. You may find this
publication helpful to answer any future questions which might arise regarding the
obligations of public officials under the Act's conflict-of-interest rules. Should factual
circumstances arising in the future cause you to have further questions regardrng a
specific conflict-of-interest issue, please do not hesitate to ask us for advice.
If you have any questions regarding matters raised in this letter, please be free to
contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel
,H{*L
Glick
By:
enneth L
Enclosure
Counsel, Legal Divi sion

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Does Ms. Georgeanne White, Chief of Staffto the Mayor, City of Fresno, have a
disqualifying conflict-of-interest under the Act prohibiting her participation in
governmental decisions concerning the Regional Medical Center Project?

CONCLUSION: Ms. White may participate in decisions concerning the Regional Medicai Center
Project if there are no reasonably foreseeable material financial effects on her economic
interests.
I Government Code $$ 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, $$ 18109-
18997, of the California Code of Regulations. As used herein "sectiorf' refers to a section of the Act and
''regulation" refers to a section or subdivision of the Commission's regulations.
2 Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or
formal written advice. (Section 83 1 1 4 ; regulation 1 83 2 9(c)(3 ).)
'gE
